zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of England於1031年11月24日出生在法國巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯凱時，她的父親Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut19歲，母親Adèle de France22歲。她於1052年與William I 'the Conqueror' King of England結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1083年11月2日在她的家鄉享年51歲，埋葬在法國巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯凱。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of England于1031年11月24日出生在法国巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯凱时，她的父亲Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut19岁，母亲Adèle de France22岁。她于1052年与William I 'the Conqueror' King of England结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1083年11月2日在她的家乡享年51岁，埋葬在法国巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯凱。

nameMeanings.name: Queen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: shortened form of   McQueen  .

English: from the Middle English female personal name Quene, sometimes a pet form of Quenill (Old English Cwēnhild, from cwēn ‘queen’ + hild ‘battle’). Early examples of the name are mainly from southern England, especially Sussex, but its currency in late medieval northern England has also been suggested by records from Yorkshire.
English: possibly a nickname from Middle English quene ‘woman; harlot; crone’ (Old English cwēn), but this is less likely than 2 to have given rise to a hereditary surname.

variants.name: McQueen
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuibhne ‘son of Suibhne’ a byname meaning ‘pleasant’. Compare McSweeney .

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuain, a Gaelic patronymic based on Old Norse sveinn ‘boy, servant’, which was also used as a personal name (see Swain ). This is given as the origin of the McQueen or McSwan family of Skye, although the spellings are hard to distinguish. Compare Whan .
Americanized form of French   Moquin  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: England
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: Mathilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Mechtild
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mechtilde
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Flanders
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, an altered form of Flanders, which originally denoted someone who came to England from that region. Its occasional use as a given name is probably in honour of the explorer Matthew Flinders ( 1774–1814 ). Flinders Petrie ( 1853–1942 ) was a famous British archaeologist.

historicEvents.title: England Divided
historicEvents.description: King Canute died in 1035 and England was divided between his three sons. His son Sweyn received Norway, Harthacanute received Denmark, and Harold Harefoot received North England.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

en.lifeSummary: When Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of England was born on 24 November 1031, in Caen, Calvados, Lower Normandy, France, her father, Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut, was 19 and her mother, Adèle de France, was 22. She married William I 'the Conqueror' King of England in 1052. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 2 November 1083, in her hometown, at the age of 51, and was buried in Caen, Calvados, Lower Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of England est née le 24 novembre 1031, à Caen, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, France, son père, Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Adèle de France, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé William I 'the Conqueror' King of England en 1052. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 2 novembre 1083, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Caen, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, France.

de.New Forest, Hampshire, England: New Forest, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of England am 24. November 1031 in Caen, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut 19 und ihre Mutter Adèle de France 22. Sie hat 1052 William I 'the Conqueror' King of England geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 2. November 1083 im Alter von 51 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Caen, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of England è nata il il 24 novembre 1031 nel Caen, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia, sua padre, Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut, aveva 19 anni e sua madre, Adèle de France, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato William I 'the Conqueror' King of England nel 1052. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 2 novembre 1083, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolta a Caen, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of Englandが1031年11月24日にフランスバセ-ノーマンディカルヴァドスケンで生まれた時，父Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainautは19歳，母Adèle de Franceは22歳であった。1052年にWilliam I 'the Conqueror' King of Englandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1083年11月2日に故郷で51歳で死去し，フランスバセ-ノーマンディカルヴァドスケンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of England(이)가 1031년 11월 24일 프랑스 배세-노먼디 캘바도스 카이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adèle de France(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1052년 William I 'the Conqueror' King of England(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1083년 11월 2일 그녀의 고향에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 배세-노먼디 캘바도스 카이에 묻혔다.

pt.24 November 1031–2 November 1083: 24 de novembro de 1031–2 de novembro de 1083
pt.New Forest, Hampshire, England: New Forest, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of England nasceu em 24 de novembro de 1031, em Caen, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, França, seu pai, Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Adèle de France, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com William I 'the Conqueror' King of England em 1052. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 2 de novembro de 1083, em sua cidade natal, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em Caen, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, França.

ro.New Forest, Hampshire, England: New Forest, Hampshire, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of England родилась 24 ноября 1031 г. в местности каен, калвадос, бассе-норманди, Франция, её отцу по имени Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Adèle de France, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William I 'the Conqueror' King of England в 1052 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 2 ноября 1083 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности каен, калвадос, бассе-норманди, Франция.

es.24 November 1031–2 November 1083: 24 de noviembre de 1031–2 de noviembre de 1083
es.New Forest, Hampshire, England: New Forest, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mathilde of Flanders Queen Consort of England nació el 24 de noviembre de 1031, en Caen, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Adèle de France, tenía 22 años. Se casó con William I 'the Conqueror' King of England en 1052. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 2 de noviembre de 1083, en su pueblo, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en Caen, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia.
